title: SwiftAPI Attestation Protocol Specification (Version 1.0.0)
author: Pal, Rayan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
SwiftAPI is a cryptographic trust authority for governing AI execution.  This specification defines the SwiftAPI Attestation Protocol (SAP), which enforces human-issued, cryptographically verifiable authority prior to the execution of AI-initiated actions. The protocol separates intelligence from permission, enabling deterministic allow/deny enforcement, scoped authorization, revocation, and post-hoc auditability for non-deterministic agent systems.  SAP is model-agnostic and framework-agnostic, and applies uniformly to any AI system capable of initiating side effects such as API calls, infrastructure changes, financial transactions, or data access. 
 https://swiftapi.ai
steps_raw:
SwiftAPI is a cryptographic trust authority for governing AI execution.  This specification defines the SwiftAPI Attestation Protocol (SAP), which enforces human-issued, cryptographically verifiable authority prior to the execution of AI-initiated actions. The protocol separates intelligence from permission, enabling deterministic allow/deny enforcement, scoped authorization, revocation, and post-hoc auditability for non-deterministic agent systems.  SAP is model-agnostic and framework-agnostic, and applies uniformly to any AI system capable of initiating side effects such as API calls, infrastructure changes, financial transactions, or data access.
